provenance.source_corpus: Biotic and Abiotic Effects of Remnant and Restoration Soils on the Performance of Tallgrass Prairie Species

source_spans.text: Soil from restored sites was more phosphorus rich, promoting greater growth of both a common and a rare prairie species when grown in intact soil cores.
Soil from restored sites was more phosphorus rich, promoting greater growth of both a common and a rare prairie species when grown in intact soil cores.
plants grown on inoculated soil had significantly greater growth... than plants grown on sterilized soil
Our results suggest that plants on restored prairies are less reliant on soil microbes due to the higher fertility found within their soils.
Our results suggest that plants on restored prairies are less reliant on soil microbes due to the higher fertility found within their soils.
Our data suggests that like other ecosystems, residual high fertility in tallgrass prairies may facilitate invasion by non-native plants.
Both high nutrient levels and lower heterogeneity can reduce diversity in plant communities
Both high nutrient levels and lower heterogeneity can reduce diversity in plant communities
In particular, increased levels of soil nutrients can decrease plant reliance on mycorrhizal interactions.
In particular, increased levels of soil nutrients can decrease plant reliance on mycorrhizal interactions.
When non-native species are less reliant on these interactions their invasiveness can be increased
the addition of organic matter to soil can dramatically lower soil nitrate levels and decrease the abundance of exotics in restored grasslands
the addition of organic matter to soil can dramatically lower soil nitrate levels and decrease the abundance of exotics in restored grasslands
the addition of organic matter to soil can dramatically lower soil nitrate levels and decrease the abundance of exotics in restored grasslands
On severely degraded soils, mycorrhizal fungi inoculation can aid in plant re-establishment
On severely degraded soils, mycorrhizal fungi inoculation can aid in plant re-establishment
The growth of big bluestem and Culver’s root were both positively correlated with mean soil P per site
The growth of Culver’s root was also positively correlated with soil inorganic N levels.
plants grown on soil from remnant sites had a BE twice as large as plants grown on soil from restoration sites
plants grown on soil from remnant sites had a BE twice as large as plants grown on soil from restoration sites
demonstrating that plants benefit more from soil biota when the soil is less fertile.
demonstrating that plants benefit more from soil biota when the soil is less fertile.
High phosphate levels have traditionally been considered to reduce plant colonization by mycorrhizal fungi
High phosphate levels have traditionally been considered to reduce plant colonization by mycorrhizal fungi
In the short term disturbances create conditions both for the establishment of exotic species and weedy species seedlings.
In the short term disturbances create conditions both for the establishment of exotic species and weedy species seedlings.
